title: rhinanthus major subsp. aestivalis
intro: Rhinanthus major subsp. aestivalis, also known as the summer rampion or yellow rampion, is a plant species in the figwort family that is native to Europe and Western Asia. This plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include its ability to stimulate digestion, reduce inflammation, and act as an antiseptic. The health benefits associated with Rhinanthus major subsp. aestivalis include its potential to relieve digestive issues such as constipation and indigestion, as well as its possible anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Rhinanthus major subsp. aestivalis can be prepared as an herbal remedy through infusion, decoction, or tincture, making it accessible in various forms for medicinal use.
